docs.name: Morphing Modal
docs.description: Modal that expands from the trigger element with spring physics and backdrop blur.

props.name: children
props.description: The payload rendering inside the physical boundaries of the modal dialog box.

props.name: className
props.description: Tailwind definitions passing specific sizes and visuals onto the dialog constraint.

props.name: layoutId
props.description: Unique arbitrary string utilized by Framer Motion to tie trigger layout transitions.

props.name: onClick
props.description: MorphingModalTrigger only: fired when the trigger is clicked — typically used to open the modal.

docs.usageCode:
"use client"; import { useState } from "react"; import { MorphingModal, MorphingModalTrigger } from "@/components/ui/morphing-modal"; export default function Example() { const [modalOpen, setModalOpen] = useState(false); return ( <>
setModalOpen(true)} className="p-6 rounded-xl bg-neutral-900/50 border border-neutral-800 hover:border-neutral-700 transition-colors max-w-xs text-white" >

Click to open modal

This card morphs into a modal with spring physics.

setModalOpen(false)} layoutId="modal-demo" >

Morphing Modal

This modal expands from the trigger element with spring-based animation. It supports Escape key to close, click-outside to dismiss, and an animated close button.

); }

docs.overview: MorphingModal animates open/close with a spring-driven scale and blur, making overlays feel fluid rather than jarring. Supports a trigger element for shared-layout animations.

scenarios.title: Confirmation dialog
scenarios.description: Use the modal for destructive actions — a focused modal prevents accidental clicks better than inline prompts.

scenarios.title: Image / media preview
scenarios.description: Expand a thumbnail into a full preview with a smooth morph transition.

scenarios.title: Multi-step onboarding modal
scenarios.description: Use internal state to drive a step-by-step flow inside a single modal instance.
